zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Annable於1360年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親Edmund Annable30歲，母親Jeannette Sanderl30歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1400年在英國享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Annable于1360年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲Edmund Annable30岁，母亲Jeannette Sanderl30岁。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1400年在英国享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Annable
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire): variant of Amable , a derivative of the Old French and Middle English female personal name Amabil, Amabel, from Latin amabilis ‘lovable’. In some cases it may also be also a habitational name from Amble, Northumberland, recorded as Amble from 1203 onward but also as Anebelle (1292) and Anebill (1347).

variants.name: Hannibal
meanings: German: from a post-humanist personal name, Hannibal (see 2 below).

English: post-medieval variant of Annable , from Middle English Amabil, Annabel. This female personal name fell out of fashion in the late Middle Ages, and the source of the surname was no longer recognized. It was re-interpreted in the 16th century by classically educated gentlemen as being the name of the Carthaginian general, Hannibal (247–182 BC ). The surname was also sometimes later re-etymologized as honey + ball or bell, hence spellings such as Honeyball and Hunnibell. In this new guise, it coincided with an established variant of Annable with initial H-.

History: There is no evidence for the use of Hannibal as a personal name in England before 1619, when Hannibal Gammon was rector of Mawgan in Pyder, Cornwall. As a medieval surname, it was borne by Matthew Hanibal (1255) and Peter Haniballus, both described as ‘civis Romanus’, and obviously Italians, but it is unlikely that either of these Italian merchants or moneylenders founded an English family.

variants.name: Amable
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French and Middle English female personal name Amabil, Amabel, Latin Amabilis ‘lovable’. The Normans frequently made use of the Old French short form Mabile or Mabel (see Mable ) and, by dissimilation of m to n, developed a new form, Anabel, which gave rise to surnames such as Annable and Hannibal .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Annable was born in 1360, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, Edmund Annable, was 30 and his mother, Jeannette Sanderl, was 30. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1400, in England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Annable est né en 1360, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Annable, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Jeannette Sanderl, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1400, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Annable 1360 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund Annable 30 und seine Mutter Jeannette Sanderl 30. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1400 im Alter von 40 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Annable è nato il nel 1360 nel London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Edmund Annable, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Jeannette Sanderl, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1400, nel England, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.London, Middlesex, England: イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Annableが1360年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Edmund Annableは30歳，母Jeannette Sanderlは30歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1400年にイギリスで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Annable(이)가 1360년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Annable(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeannette Sanderl(은)는 30세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1400년 영국에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Annable nasceu em 1360, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Annable, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Jeannette Sanderl, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1400, em Inglaterra, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Annable родился в 1360 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Annable было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeannette Sanderl, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1400 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Annable nació en 1360, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Annable, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Jeannette Sanderl, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1400, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años.
